sourceSectionFilterAuditRows.rawLine: Nanoscience and Molecular Engineering (NME) option: The Nanoscience and Molecular Engineering (NME) Option in the Bachelor of Science in Chemical Engineering is a transcripted option focusing on nanoscale principles in molecular engineering that provides hands-on experience and stresses the interdisciplinary nature of the field. To declare the NME option, students must complete NME 220 and CHEM E 310, which are generally taken together in spring quarter of their sophomore year. For more information about the NME option for the B.S. ChemE, please see our NME Option page.

supportLists.listTitle: Scoped section: The minimum number of credits required for a B.S.Ch.E degree is 180. Some courses cannot be counted toward degree requir

requirementCueLines: CHEM E 480 (4): Process Dynamics and Control
CHEM E 455 (3): Surface and Colloid Lab OR CHEM E 460 (3): Polymers Lab

Standard option: Any 16 credits listed on the Engineering Electives page. Students may also petition for other engineering courses to count. Most courses from outside the College of Engineering do not count.

Nanoscience and Molecular Engineering (NME) option: The Nanoscience and Molecular Engineering (NME) Option in the Bachelor of Science in Chemical Engineering is a transcripted option focusing on nanoscale principles in molecular engineering that provides hands-on experience and stresses the interdisciplinary nature of the field. To declare the NME option, students must complete NME 220 and CHEM E 310, which are generally taken together in spring quarter of their sophomore year. For more information about the NME option for the B.S. ChemE, please see our NME Option page.

Optional focus areas: Students may choose to complete an optional focus area. These focus areas are not listed on the transcript. Many courses that count for the focus areas also count as engineering electives. For a list of focus areas, please see our Optional Focus Areas page.
The total number of credits needed for graduation is 180. If additional credits are needed, students can take additional electives to reach the credit requirement.
chooseStatements: Optional focus areas: Students may choose to complete an optional focus area. These focus areas are not listed on the transcript. Many courses that count for the focus areas also count as engineering electives. For a list of focus areas, please see our Optional Focus Areas page.
